title: MD
biography: Willem Kolff was a Dutch-American physician who invented the first practical artificial kidney dialysis machine in 1943 during the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands. He is also a pioneer of the artificial heart and is considered the father of artificial organs.
aiSummary: Willem Kolff was a nephrology specialist who invented the artificial kidney dialysis machine in 1943. Father of artificial organs. Based in Salt Lake City, United States.

citations.title: The artificial kidney: a dialyser with a great area

awards: Albert Lasker Award for Clinical Medical Research

timeline.title: Built first artificial kidney
timeline.description: Constructed a dialysis machine from sausage casings and orange juice cans during the Nazi occupation

timeline.title: First successful dialysis
timeline.description: Saved the life of a 67-year-old woman using his artificial kidney

timeline.title: Joined Cleveland Clinic
timeline.description: Established artificial organs department and continued innovating

timeline.title: Moved to University of Utah
timeline.description: Led development of the artificial heart program that led to the Jarvik heart
